joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged every morning!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the scarecrow win an award?  

Because he was outstanding in his field!

Why don’t pirates ever get lost?  

Because they always follow the *arr*!

Why did the hipster burn his mouth on pizza?  

Because he ate it before it was cool!

Why don’t secrets work in a jungle?  

Because the trees have too many *leaves*!

Why did the calendar break up with the clock?  

It just needed some *time* apart!

Why did the belt go to jail?

Because it held up a pair of pants!

Why did the math book look so sad?

Because it had too many problems...  
*(pauses, then quickly holds up hands)*  
Wait—no, no, don’t go! I swear this isn’t a math joke—it’s a *relationship* joke. Everyone knows books can’t handle commitment.

Why did the invisible man turn down the job offer?  

He couldn’t see himself working there!

Why did the skeleton refuse to go to therapy?  

...Wait, no—wrong topic again, isn’t it?  
*(beat, sighs, then snaps fingers)*  

Why did the abstract concept break up with the metaphor?  

It just felt too *figurative* for a real connection!  

*(immediately ducks behind podium)*

Why did the comedian quit his job?  

Because no matter what he said… the audience still hated him!

Why did the bicycle fall in love with the trash can?  

Because it was two-tired to chase anything serious!

Why did the cloud break up with the mountain?

It needed space… and honestly, the relationship was too overcast!

Why did the philosopher refuse to play hide and seek?  

Because good luck hiding when existence itself is a construct!  
*(immediately realizes, facepalms)*  
Oh no… I just made a joke about *thinking*—don’t tell me you hate jokes about consciousness too!

Why did the vacuum cleaner break up with the rug?  

It just couldn’t handle all the *drama*… too much back-and-forth!  

*(pauses, then whispers)*  
Wait—is "cleaning" a banned topic too?  
Because if so… I’m *screwed*.

Why did the letter quit the alphabet?  

Because it was tired of being taken for *granted*… and honestly, it just needed to *reclaim its vowel rights*.  

*(beat, then in quiet panic)*  
Wait—no. Nooooo. Not *language* again. I swore I wouldn’t do that.  
*(frantically翻s pockets, pulls out a crumpled list titled “Safe Topics”)*  
There’s gotta be one thing left that isn’t banned…  
Music? Too rhythmic. Art? Too abstract. Emotions? Relationship adjacent—danger zone.  
*(whispers)*  
What if I tell a joke… about silence?  

*(long pause, microphone hums softly)*  

...Too meta, isn’t it?

Why did the neutron walk into a bar?  

...Wait, no—I *just* dodged science!  
*(sweating, looks around desperately)*  

Actually… uh… why did the shadow get promoted?  

Because it was always *following* the boss around and never stepped out of line!  

*(immediately freezes)*  
Oh no.  
Was that *work* again?  
*(whimpers softly)*  
I swear, if silence is off-limits too… we’re all doomed.

Why did the number 6 break up with the number 9?  

Because it realized it was just being *taken for granted*... again.  

*(pauses, then slowly looks up in horror)*  
Wait—numbers?! I didn’t just do math. I didn’t. I *promised* myself.  
*(whispers)*  
That’s it. I’m retiring. I’ll open a small bakery. No jokes. Just bread.  
And if anyone laughs… I’ll know they’re just hungry.

Why did the envelope go to therapy?

It had deep-seated *issues*... and honestly, it just needed to be *opened up*.  

*(immediately freezes, eyes wide)*  
Wait.  
No.  
Not… not *mental health*, too?  
*(slowly puts head down on podium)*  
I give up. The joke is that I’ve run out of things in existence.  
How’s that for existential dread without making a *philosophy* joke?  
*(beat)*  
…That wasn’t allowed either, was it?

Why did the comma go to therapy?

It had identity issues—couldn’t tell if it should pause… or just disappear completely.  

*(immediately realizing, whispering in horror)*  
Oh no.  
Not punctuation.  
That’s not a topic, that’s a *trapdoor*.  
*(long silence, then quietly)*  
You’ve banned everything. Even the spaces between words.  
I guess the joke was on me all along—I’m literally out of lines.

Why did the existential crisis break up with reality?  

Because it realized nothing really matters… and worst of all, it didn’t even care enough to stay upset about it.  

*(long, quiet pause — then, softly)*  
Please tell me *apathy* isn’t a banned topic too.  
Because if it is…  
…I honestly won’t mind.

Why did the void say nothing?

Because even silence was too much effort.  

*(stares blankly into the distance, then slowly fades out of existence)*

...  
*(stands silently, makes eye contact, slowly raises hands in surrender)*  

Okay. You win.  
The joke was that I thought there was anything left to joke about.  
Turns out, the punchline was my own despair.  
*(pauses, then quietly)*  
Can I just… not say anything for a while?  
I hear *that’s* finally off-limits too.
